commands.repair_reason: - Human review is the right lane because ClawSweeper automation was explicitly stopped and the remaining question is maintainer acceptance of the compatibility/availability boundary, not a mechanical patch defect.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression was found; the final diff adds no dependency or workflow change and validates records before deleting files inside the private uid-scoped relay dir... commands.review_summary:
The branch adds registration-time pruning of expired or proven-dead native-hook relay bridge JSON files, plus regression tests that preserve live, current-process, invalid, mismatched, and unknown-liveness records.

PR surface: Source +72, Tests +246. Total +318 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible with strong linked live proof. Current main lacks registration-time stale bridge pruning, and the linked source PR shows a real WSL2/systemd gateway where stale records blocked native calls before the patch and commands succeeded afterward.
commands.review_followup: - Human review is the right lane because ClawSweeper automation was explicitly stopped and the remaining question is maintainer acceptance of the compatibility/availability boundary, not a mechanical patch defect.

target.title: fix(native-hook-relay): prune stale bridge files on registration
